senses.gloss: verbal noun of نَوَى (nawā, “to think, to intend”) (form I)

examples.text: إِنَّمَا الْأَعْمَالُ بِالنِّيَّاتِ، وَإِنَّمَا لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِئٍ مَا نَوَى
examples.english: But deeds are in intentions, and every man will have what he intends.

senses.gloss: will, tendency, inclination, wish, object of investigation

etymology.text: Compare Classical Syriac ܢܹܐ (nē, “please”) and Hebrew נָא (ná, “please (adverb), if you please”).
